steps.title: Identify what the question asks for
steps.content: The question asks which choice "emphasizes a difference between the two movies." The key facts from the notes are: The Last Moment is lost (no copies exist), while Within Our Gates is archived at UCLA. Any correct answer must directly contrast these two fates.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• "While Paul Fejos's *The Last Moment* is considered lost to history, Oscar Micheaux's film *Within Our Gates* is archived at the UCLA Film & Television Archive in Los Angeles, California.": The word "While" explicitly contrasts lost versus archived, placing both films in direct comparison. ✓
  • "Paul Fejos's *The Last Moment* (1928) and Oscar Micheaux's *Within Our Gates* (1920) are just two examples of silent films from the 1920s.": This treats both films as similar examples of the same category; it emphasizes similarity, not difference. ✗
  • "Oscar Micheaux's film *Within Our Gates* can be found at the UCLA Film & Television Archive in Los Angeles, California.": This mentions only one film, so no comparison between the two is possible. ✗
  • "Paul Fejos's 1928 film *The Last Moment* is among the many lost films of the era.": This mentions only one film and omits Within Our Gates entirely, preventing any contrast. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
